title: Beginning in Bend
body:
Been a month since leaving White Salmon, WA to set out on this adventure. Still calling Washington home for now, but really the road is going to be home this spring and summer (though Alaska is always my real home, and our eventual destination this summer). 

The Alaskan winters finally got me to leave in 2022 to try something a little warmer in the region, close to fam and friends. After a stint in Portland, two stints in White Salmon (an unforgettable adventure or two on of their own), it's time to embark on an epic roadtrip with Wojo (the star and voice of this journal hereafter).

We'll be exploring the southwest, playing it by ear, and trying to save money to eventually land somewhere more permanent. We'll log our travels and experiences here as often as we can, but not too much. Next stop will be back up in The Gorge to take care of some business and see fam before really heading out.

The site and our voices will evolve over time. Please reach out directly if you know me or via the anonymous form in the Wandering Wojo panel on the main page here. 

Wish us luck! Hit us up! Send suggestions, essential albums, or whatever else you think might enhance the experience. We're excited, a little nervous, but really ready to have the roadtrip of a lifetime!

title: Touching Base
body:
Started feeling a bit antsy in Central Oregon… Ready to get on the road but taking care of odds and ends, errands, paperwork, setting up the truck, all while he’s working full time and we’re hopping from place to place, crashing guest rooms.. odd how this can feel claustrophobic. 

Felt amazing to get on the road, even if just on a trip back north to The Gorge. Just a tiny taste of what’s to come. Half-way through the ride I got a little restless, stuck in this mesh backpack the big guy got me. He let me out and holy shit… sounds, trees and lights moving faster than I ever realized they could. He let me sit on his lap towards the end of the ride while he sang with the big sounds, “You’ve got a friend… ‘aint it good to know…”

Now it’s just biding time in the hot room upstairs while it rains outside. I trust he’ll get our space all fixed up for the next leg of the ride. Meanwhile, I think I can see that he’s got some big feelings, maybe some sadness being back where he had his biggest dreams and almost made it all come together, but not quite.

I can see his eyes get watery sometimes, but I know he’s been through tough times and can handle this. He says his shoulder tat is about flexibility, agility, resilience… “Whatever way you throw him, he lands upright”. Anyway… kinda like me, I guess. Being a cat and all.

Still, I can hear him laughing and chatting with the fam he has here and knows it puts him at ease, maybe reminding him that it’s not just me who cares and looks after him. Seems like he loves them as much as I love him. Ugh, what a swirl of feelings. Let’s hope he finds some ease and smiles… maybe recharges a bit so we can start this adventure on the right paw.
